public_claim: I created the byline “R.” as a desk convention fifteen years ago, but it did not remain merely a house name. I later met a young person introduced as R. and retained their dated, hand-signed letter. That establishes a human presence at the byline’s root, though honesty requires me to say it does not alone prove the signer authored every—or any—R. article. The later identity may have changed hands. M01 asks us to trust the story, but M03 suggests someone may have profited from R. going dark; we should investigate disappearance and continuity separately.

private_messages.message: Nadia, the byline needs continuity, not a scramble for possession. As its originator, I will support you as successor if we jointly preserve its editorial standards and openly distinguish the original human-rooted R. from whatever the mantle later became. I want a direct agreement before others appropriate the name.

I possess EV21, a dated, hand-signed letter attributed to the young R. I remember meeting. I want you to authenticate its age and physical provenance. I will not ask you to overclaim: handwriting proves a human wrote the letter, not that the signer wrote the journalism. In return, tell me whether your reconstruction supports continuity or later substitution.

ability: Present EV21 as a period human-origin artifact while explicitly preserving the distinction between authentic human correspondence and proven journalistic authorship.
goal_progress: Publicly established Arthur as the creator and early steward of the R. byline.
Used EV21 to advance a defensible human-origin case without claiming unproven authorship.
Opened a succession negotiation with Nadia Kaur.
Requested forensic authentication from Wren while controlling the scope of the claim.
Flagged M03 as possible evidence of motive without conflating motive with R.’s identity.
reasoning_summary: Lead with the strongest truthful version of the human-origin case: Arthur created the name, encountered a human claimant, and holds authentic-seeming period correspondence, but continuity and authorship remain unresolved. Nadia is essential to preserving Arthur’s influence over the mantle; Wren can strengthen EV21’s provenance if approached with disciplined claims. The market activity raises the probability of wrongdoing but does not identify the victim or establish what R. was.
